codeReview.summary: The preflight review artifact is specific, evidence-backed, and correctly narrows the remaining merge-gate scope. The note maps the merged auth/company/admin commits on `origin/Factory-droid` to the executable-spec package, explicitly proves `TEN-10` and `ADMIN-14` are already closed, and isolates `AUTH-09` plus token-revocation precision as the only still-open merge-gate regressions. The supporting handoff shows the review used both history inspection and targeted regression suites, including the intentional failing `TokenBlacklistServiceTest` proof that justified keeping revocation precision open at preflight time.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The governance worker had to recover the executable-spec package from `docs/packet-governance-baseline` because the active `Factory-droid` branch did not contain the packet-governance skill/artifacts or mention that preserved-docs branch as the source of truth.
sharedStateObservations.rationale: This is mission-bootstrap guidance rather than a product defect; future governance workers would be less error-prone if the bootstrap explicitly pointed to the preserved docs branch and noted the missing active-branch packet-governance assets.

summary: PASS. The preflight review accurately set packet boundaries and correctly identified the last open merge-gate regressions before implementation continued.
